components.description: Unified QC step that routes data based on sequencing type: Illumina/Ion reads are processed with FastQC, while Nanopore reads are processed with NanoPlot and a custom reads check. Use this step to assess raw read quality before any trimming or assembly.
use_cases: Check the quality of my raw sequencing reads
Run QC on my FASTQ files before trimming
Assess sequencing quality for Illumina or Nanopore data
Generate quality reports and read statistics

components.description: Comprehensive preprocessing workflow that performs: 1) Quality trimming with fastp, 2) Post-trimming QC with FastQC, and 3) Consistency checks using custom Python scripts. Optimized for Illumina paired-end and Ion Torrent short reads. Removes adapter sequences, trims low-quality bases, and validates read integrity.
use_cases: Trim adapter sequences from my Illumina paired-end reads
Preprocess my raw sequencing data before assembly
Remove low quality bases from my FASTQ files
Clean up raw short-read data with fastp

components.description: Legacy trimming workflow optimized for Illumina and Ion Torrent data. Performs: 1) Trimming with Trimmomatic, 2) Post-trim QC with FastQC, 3) Reads consistency check. Preferred for non-bacterial Illumina samples where fastp is not the default.
use_cases: Trim my Illumina reads with Trimmomatic
Remove adapters and low quality bases using legacy trimmer
Preprocess non-bacterial Illumina paired-end data

components.description: Long-read preprocessing workflow for Oxford Nanopore data. Performs: 1) Filtering/Trimming with Chopper (Rust implementation of NanoFilt), 2) Post-trimming QC with NanoPlot. Filters reads by quality score, minimum/maximum length, and can crop heads/tails.
use_cases: Trim my Nanopore long reads
Filter long reads by quality and length
Preprocess Oxford Nanopore sequencing data
Remove low quality Nanopore reads before assembly

components.description: Host depletion workflow for short reads. 1) Maps reads against a host reference using Bowtie2. 2) Uses Samtools to filter and retain ONLY the unmapped (non-host) reads, converting them back to FastQ format. Essential for removing human or animal DNA contamination from clinical/environmental samples.
use_cases: Remove host reads from my sample
Deplete human DNA from my sequencing data
Filter out host contamination before assembly
Keep only non-host microbial reads

components.description: Host depletion workflow specifically for Nanopore long reads. 1) Maps reads against a host reference using Minimap2 (preset: map-ont). 2) Uses Samtools to filter and retain ONLY the unmapped reads, converting them back to FastQ. Use this when processing long-read clinical or environmental samples with host contamination.
use_cases: Remove host reads from my Nanopore data
Deplete host DNA from long read sequencing
Filter out host contamination from Nanopore reads

components.description: Positive Selection workflow for short reads. 1) Maps reads against a specific reference genome using Bowtie2. 2) Uses Samtools to RETAIN only the reads that mapped successfully (discarding unmapped reads), converting them back to FastQ format. Useful for enriching target organism sequences from complex or metagenomic samples.
use_cases: Keep only reads that map to my reference genome
Filter reads to retain target organism sequences
Positive selection of pathogen reads from a clinical sample
Enrich specific sequences from a metagenomic sample

components.description: Taxonomic filtering utility. Uses the output from a PREVIOUS Kraken classification (Report + Output) to filter the original trimmed reads, retaining only those belonging to a specific NCBI Taxonomy ID (and optionally its parents/children). Allows targeted extraction of reads assigned to a specific organism or clade.
use_cases: Extract reads belonging to a specific taxonomic group
Filter reads by NCBI Taxonomy ID
Retain only reads classified as a specific species or genus
Isolate reads from a target organism after Kraken classification

components.description: Positive Selection workflow for Nanopore long reads. 1) Maps reads against a specific reference genome using Minimap2 (preset: map-ont). 2) Uses Samtools to RETAIN only the reads that mapped successfully, converting them back to FastQ format. Use for enriching target pathogen sequences from complex long-read samples.
use_cases: Keep only Nanopore reads that map to my reference
Filter long reads for a specific pathogen
Positive selection on Nanopore data against a reference genome

components.description: Normalizes read depth (coverage) to reduce computational load for downstream assembly. Specifically configured for paired-end Illumina data using k-mer based normalization. Reduces excessive coverage to a target depth, improving assembly speed without sacrificing quality.
use_cases: Normalize read depth before assembly
Reduce excessive coverage in my dataset
Downsample high-coverage Illumina data
Speed up assembly by reducing redundant reads
components.notes: Script explicitly handles Java heap memory calculation (-Xmx) based on 85% of available container memory.

components.description: Synthetic FastQ generation from Fasta files. Uses a custom Python script to create artificial quality scores and generate paired-end reads from an assembly or consensus sequence. Enables re-analysis of assemblies through standard read-based pipelines.
use_cases: Generate synthetic FASTQ reads from a FASTA assembly
Convert my consensus sequence to paired-end reads
Create artificial reads from an assembly for re-analysis

components.description: Accurate de novo assembly workflow for short reads. Performs: 1) Assembly with SPAdes (optimized for Illumina or Ion Torrent), 2) Post-assembly filtering (removing scaffolds < 200bp), and 3) Quality assessment with QUAST. The standard assembler for general-purpose genome reconstruction from short reads.
use_cases: Assemble my reads de novo without a reference genome
Build a genome from Illumina short reads
Create contigs and scaffolds from paired-end data
De novo assembly with automatic quality assessment

components.description: Fast assembly pipeline wrapping SPAdes/Velvet, optimized for bacterial isolates. Includes distinct paths for Illumina Paired-End (Shovill) and Ion Torrent (Shovill-SE), followed by QC with QUAST and optional genome completeness assessment with CheckM. Best choice for rapid bacterial isolate assembly.
use_cases: Quickly assemble a bacterial isolate genome
Fast de novo assembly for bacteria
Assemble my bacterial whole genome sequencing data
Get a quick bacterial assembly with completeness check

components.description: Short-read assembly optimizer workflow for bacterial genomes. Performs: 1) Assembly using Unicycler (SPAdes optimizer), 2) Post-assembly filtering (<200bp), and 3) Quality control with QUAST. Produces more contiguous assemblies than raw SPAdes, especially for circular bacterial chromosomes.
use_cases: Assemble my bacterial genome with better contiguity
Optimize my short-read assembly for bacteria
Get a more complete bacterial genome assembly

components.description: Specialized pipeline for extracting and assembling plasmids from sequencing data. Performs: 1) Plasmid-specific assembly with SPAdes (Illumina or Ion Torrent), 2) Specialized filtering using AssemblyFilterPlasmids.py, and 3) Quality control with QUAST. Targets mobile genetic elements and extrachromosomal DNA.
use_cases: Extract and assemble plasmids from my sequencing data
Identify plasmid sequences in my bacterial sample
Assemble mobile genetic elements separately
Reconstruct plasmid sequences from short reads

components.description: Hybrid assembly workflow combining Short Reads (Illumina) for base-level accuracy and Long Reads (Nanopore) for structural continuity. Performs: 1) Hybrid assembly with Unicycler, 2) Quality control with QUAST. Produces the most complete assemblies by leveraging both sequencing technologies.
use_cases: Combine my Illumina and Nanopore reads for assembly
Hybrid assembly for better genome completeness
Use both short and long reads to assemble my genome
Get a complete bacterial genome using hybrid sequencing

components.description: Reference-based consensus calling workflow. Performs: 1) Alignment with Bowtie2, 2) Consensus generation using Samtools (legacy) and BioPython, 3) Comprehensive coverage analysis (depth, min/max stats), and 4) Visualization with Matplotlib.
use_cases: Align Illumina or Ion Torrent reads to a reference genome
Generate a consensus sequence using Bowtie2
Map short reads and calculate genome coverage depth

components.description: Reference-based consensus calling workflow for Nanopore long reads. Performs: 1) Alignment with Minimap2 (map-ont), 2) Consensus generation using Samtools (legacy) and BioPython, 3) Coverage depth analysis, and 4) Visualization with Matplotlib.
use_cases: Align Nanopore long reads to a reference genome
Generate consensus from Nanopore data using Minimap2
Map ONT reads and calculate coverage statistics

components.description: Rapid variant calling and core genome alignment pipeline. Supports Illumina Paired-end reads, Ion Torrent reads, and pre-assembled Contigs (FASTA mode) against a reference genome (FASTA or GenBank).
use_cases: Call variants from Illumina or Ion Torrent reads against a reference
Perform rapid SNP calling with Snippy
Align assembled contigs to a reference genome

components.description: Viral consensus calling workflow. Performs: 1) Alignment using Snippy, 2) Pileup generation with Samtools, 3) Consensus calling with iVar (trimming primers/quality). Supports Segmented Genomes (e.g., Influenza) by aggregating consensus sequences from multiple reference segments.
use_cases: Generate a consensus sequence for viral genomes
Process segmented viruses like Influenza
Trim primers and call variants with iVar
components.notes: Includes logic for calculating Execution Dates (DT) based on reference index for segmented viruses.

components.description: Nanopore consensus polishing and variant calling workflow. Performs: 1) Consensus generation using Medaka (neural network-based), 2) Coverage depth analysis using Samtools and custom scripts, and 3) Visualization of coverage plots.
use_cases: Polish Nanopore consensus sequences
Run neural network-based variant calling for ONT data
Improve Nanopore assembly accuracy with Medaka

components.description: Post-assembly filtering utility using a custom Python script (`cleanDenovo.py`). It filters de novo assembly contigs based on variant calls and reference alignment. **Note:** Only executes if the reference genome is located within the `FILTERABLE_REFERENCES_PATH` (typically a Viral DB). Cleans up assembly artifacts and removes spurious contigs.
use_cases: Filter my de novo assembly contigs for quality
Clean up assembly based on variant calls and reference alignment
Remove low-quality or spurious contigs from viral assembly
components.notes: The helper script location is derived from the '2AS_denovo__spades' directory in the code, despite the step ID being 'filtering'.

components.description: Metagenomic assembly workflow for mixed microbial communities. Performs: 1) Assembly using metaSPAdes (k-mers 21,33,55,77), 2) Post-assembly filtering (<200bp) using a custom Python script, and 3) Quality assessment with QUAST. Designed for reconstructing genomes from complex environmental or clinical metagenomes.
use_cases: Assemble a metagenomic sample with multiple species
Build contigs from a mixed microbial community
De novo assembly of microbiome sequencing data
Reconstruct genomes from environmental metagenomes

components.description: Legacy taxonomic classification workflow. Performs: 1) Classification with Kraken 1.0, 2) Abundance estimation with Bracken (Genus & Species levels), and 3) Output formatting for system import. **Note:** Automatically skipped for SARS-CoV-2, Control, and 16S samples. Use Kraken2 for newer analyses.
use_cases: Classify the taxonomy of my sequencing reads
Identify what species are in my sample
Estimate microbial abundance at genus and species level

components.description: Standard taxonomic classification workflow. Performs: 1) Classification with Kraken2, 2) Abundance re-estimation with Bracken (Species and Genus level), and 3) Format conversion for system import. The recommended classifier for most short-read taxonomic profiling tasks.
use_cases: Classify my reads taxonomically with Kraken2
Identify species composition of my sample
Perform taxonomic profiling of metagenomic reads
Estimate species and genus abundance in my data
components.notes: Requires a valid Kraken2 database path passed via parameters.

components.description: Long-read taxonomic classification workflow for Nanopore data. Performs: 1) Classification with Centrifuge (optimized for Nanopore), 2) Generation of Genus/Species specific reports, 3) Conversion to Kraken-like reports, and 4) Formatting for system import. The only taxonomic classifier specifically designed for long reads.
use_cases: Classify my Nanopore reads taxonomically
Identify species from long read sequencing data
Perform taxonomic classification on Nanopore reads
components.notes: Code explicitly restricts execution to Nanopore reads via 'isCompatibleWithSeqType(reads, ['nanopore'])'.

components.description: Detects intra-species bacterial contamination in raw reads. Uses core genome MLST (cgMLST) to identify multiple strains mixed in a single sample. **Constraint:** Only runs if the genus is 'Escherichia', 'Salmonella', or 'Listeria'. Essential for confirming sample purity before epidemiological analysis.
use_cases: Check my sample for intra-species contamination
Detect if multiple strains are mixed in my sample
Verify sample purity for Salmonella, Listeria, or E. coli
components.notes: Logic enforces 'isSpeciesSupported' check against a hardcoded allowlist: [escherichia, salmonella, listeria].

components.description: Predicts bacterial and viral species using a K-mer based template search. Runs `FindTemplate.py` against both Bacterial and Viral databases, then parses the results to assign a definitive species and reference genome path. Key step for driving downstream typing and mapping tools.
use_cases: Predict the species of my sample
Identify bacterial or viral species using k-mers
Find the best reference genome for my organism
Assign species identity to drive downstream analysis
components.notes: Logic extracts 'speciesAssigned' and 'assembly_accBacteria' from the output check file to drive downstream steps.

components.description: Mass screening of contigs for viral species using predefined databases. Specifically configured to use the 'viruses_TREF' database located in the mounted `abricateDBDir`. Rapid identification of viral sequences in assembled contigs.
use_cases: Screen my contigs for viral sequences
Identify viral species from my assembly
Detect viruses in my assembled sequences
components.notes: Workflow automatically injects 'viruses_TREF' as the database argument. Requires explicit mounting of the database directory.

components.description: Fast genome distance estimation using MinHash sketches. Runs `mash screen` against a pre-packaged RefSeq database (`refseq.genomes.k21s1000.msh`) and assesses results using `assess_mash_screen.py` to determine Gram stain and species identity. No external database mounting required.
use_cases: Rapidly estimate which species my sample belongs to
Check genome distance against RefSeq database
Determine Gram stain type of my bacteria
Quick species ID without a dedicated classifier
components.notes: Uses internal container paths for RefSeq DB and Gram stain resources. Does NOT require external DB mounting.

components.description: Predicts antibiotic resistance genes from raw reads. Runs `run_resfinder.py` with specific thresholds (L=0.6, T=0.9) against the acquired resistance database. Identifies acquired antimicrobial resistance determinants in bacterial samples.
use_cases: Find antimicrobial resistance genes in my sample
Detect antibiotic resistance from sequencing data
Screen my reads for AMR genes
Identify acquired resistance determinants
components.notes: Uses internal container database at `/resfinder/db_resfinder`. Requires 'genus_species' parameter formatted with underscores (e.g., Escherichia_coli).

components.description: Scans assembled contigs for AMR genes and point mutations using StarAMR. **Constraint:** Currently restricted to 'Campylobacter' species via the `isSpeciesSupported` check. It dynamically maps the genus to the correct PointFinder organism parameter. Detects both gene-based and mutation-based resistance.
use_cases: Scan my assembly for AMR genes and point mutations
Detect resistance in Campylobacter isolates
Screen for antimicrobial resistance genes in my assembly
components.notes: Logic enforces a hardcoded allowlist: [campylobacter]. Requires 'genus_species' to resolve the '--pointfinder-organism' flag.

components.description: Mass screening of assembled contigs against the Virulence Factor Database (VFDB). Generates CSV reports, detailed calls, and a summary of virulence factors found. Identifies pathogenicity-associated genes in bacterial genomes.
use_cases: Screen my contigs for virulence factors
Detect virulence genes in my bacterial assembly
Find virulence factor database hits in my genome
components.notes: Hardcoded to use '--db vfdb'. Does not support dynamic database selection in this specific module configuration.

components.description: Post-processing logic to filter Abricate results based on Coverage and Identity thresholds. **Note:** The filtering logic is embedded directly in the Nextflow script as an inline Python block. Removes low-confidence hits to reduce false positives.
use_cases: Filter Abricate results by coverage and identity thresholds
Post-process AMR screening results to remove low-confidence hits
Apply quality filters to virulence or resistance hits
components.notes: Reads column 9 (Coverage) and 10 (Identity) from Abricate output. Does NOT require an external script file.

components.description: Functional genome annotation pipeline for predicting genes, proteins, and functional elements. Supports Bacteria, Viruses, Archaea, and Mitochondria. Can optionally use a reference GenBank file (`--proteins`) to transfer annotations. Generates GFF, GenBank, and FASTA output files.
use_cases: Annotate my genome assembly
Predict genes in my bacterial or viral genome
Generate GFF and GenBank annotation files
Functionally annotate my assembled genome
components.notes: Kingdom is validated against an allowed Enum: [Viruses, Bacteria, Archaea, Mitochondria]. Reference GenBank input is optional.

components.description: Multi Locus Sequence Typing (MLST) workflow for bacterial strain classification. Performs: 1) ST assignment using `mlst` tool (with optional schema exclusion), 2) Result processing and Clonal Complex (CC) assignment using a custom Python script. Standard method for bacterial epidemiological typing.
use_cases: Determine the MLST sequence type of my bacterial isolate
Assign a clonal complex to my sample
Type my bacterial genome using MLST
Classify my bacterial strain for epidemiological tracking
components.notes: Helper script relies on internal container path `/NGStools/mlst/db/pubmlst` to map STs to Clonal Complexes.

components.description: Core/Whole Genome MLST (cgMLST/wgMLST) workflow for high-resolution bacterial typing. Performs: 1) Allele calling using ChewBBACA with pre-loaded schemas (Listeria, E. coli, Salmonella), 2) Quality checks on called alleles, and 3) CRC32 hashing of the allelic profile for anonymized sharing. Used for outbreak investigation and surveillance.
use_cases: Perform cgMLST allele calling on my assembly
Type my isolate using core genome MLST
Generate an allelic profile for cluster analysis
High-resolution typing for outbreak investigation
components.notes: Restricted to specific species (Listeria, E. coli, Salmonella) via internal schema maps. Automatically adjusts parameters for Ion Torrent data.

components.description: Infers the flaA locus variant for Campylobacter samples. Uses the standard `mlst` tool but forces the schema to 'flaA'. Includes post-processing to map results to clonal complexes. Species-specific typing for Campylobacter epidemiology.
use_cases: Type the flaA locus of my Campylobacter isolate
Determine flaA variant for Campylobacter
Campylobacter-specific molecular typing
components.notes: Strictly restricted to 'campylobacter' via 'isSpeciesSupported'. Uses internal container path `/NGStools/mlst/db/pubmlst` for post-processing.

components.description: SARS-CoV-2 Lineage assignment using Pangolin. Assigns Pango lineages to query SARS-CoV-2 consensus sequences. Essential for COVID-19 surveillance and variant tracking.
use_cases: Assign a Pango lineage to my SARS-CoV-2 sequence
Determine the lineage of my COVID sample
Type my SARS-CoV-2 consensus sequence
Track COVID-19 variants in my samples
components.notes: Uses a custom container with specific pango-data versions (v4.3.1-v0.1.12-v0.3.17-v1.21). Requires root user execution.

components.description: West Nile Virus (WNV) lineage assignment workflow. Performs: 1) Alignment of reads against multiple reference genomes (defined in `westnile_lineage_references.json`) using Snippy, 2) Coverage depth calculation with Samtools, and 3) Lineage determination using a custom Python script based on coverage thresholds. Key tool for arbovirus surveillance.
use_cases: Determine the lineage of my West Nile Virus sample
Assign WNV lineage from sequencing data
Type my West Nile Virus isolate
components.notes: Relies on an external JSON file (`westnile_lineage_references.json`) in the assets directory to define the reference panel. Uses a mapping-based approach rather than a classifier tool.

components.description: Reconstruction and typing of plasmid sequences from assembly contigs. Uses `mob_recon` to identify and reconstruct plasmids.
use_cases: Identify and reconstruct plasmids from assembly contigs
Type bacterial plasmids
Extract plasmids from Unicycler assemblies
components.notes: Runs `mob_recon` with `--unicycler_contigs` and `--run_overhang` flags. Expects input assembly to be potentially generated by Unicycler.

components.description: Core genome SNP calling pipeline. Aligns sequence reads (Illumina paired-end, IonTorrent single-end) or direct FASTA assemblies against a reference using Snippy, then aggregates multiple VCF results into a core-SNP matrix using Snippy-Core.
use_cases: Create a core SNP alignment from multiple Snippy runs
Aggregate VCF files into a multi-sample alignment
Build a core genome SNP matrix for phylogeny
components.notes: Automatically toggles parameters for single vs paired end reads, and accepts pre-computed VCF directories to run Snippy-Core exclusively.

components.description: Constructs a pangenome graph from annotated GFF files. Filters out invalid genes, performs core genome alignment using MAFFT, and resolves paralogs. Suitable for large multi-sample bacterial cohorts.
use_cases: Construct a pangenome graph from GFF annotations
Generate a core genome alignment with Panaroo
Analyze gene presence and absence across multiple genomes
components.notes: Strictly utilizes MAFFT for the core alignment phase. Tolerates custom thresholds for clustering stringency.

components.description: Complete Nextstrain/Augur phylogenetic pipeline. Performs: 1) Metadata standardization, 2) Multi-sequence alignment, 3) Tree building & refinement (TimeTree/Coalescent), 4) Ancestral state reconstruction (nucleotide & amino acid), 5) Trait inference (Geography), and 6) Export to Auspice JSON format.
use_cases: Build a phylogenetic tree with Nextstrain tools
Perform ancestral state reconstruction on a tree
Generate Auspice JSON files for tree visualization
components.notes: Automatically standardizes metadata columns (renaming to 'name' and 'date') before processing. Requires a GenBank reference for feature translation.

components.description: Multi-sample clustering and surveillance workflow. Dynamically routes execution to one of three sub-pipelines based on input type: 1) Alleles (cgMLST profiles), 2) Alignment (FASTA MSA), or 3) VCF (SNP calls). Generates Minimum Spanning Trees (MST) and metadata-enriched visualizations.
use_cases: Cluster multiple samples based on alleles, alignments, or VCFs
Generate a Minimum Spanning Tree for outbreak surveillance
Dynamically route clustering tasks based on the provided input type
components.notes: Acts as a router. Requires `multi_clustering__reportree__input` parameter to be one of ['alleles', 'alignment', 'vcf'] to select the correct logic path.

components.description: Multi-sample clustering and surveillance workflow. Dynamically routes execution to one of three sub-pipelines based on input type: 1) **Alleles** (cgMLST profiles), 2) **Alignment** (FASTA MSA), or 3) **VCF** (SNP calls). Generates Minimum Spanning Trees (MST) and metadata-enriched visualizations.
components.notes: Acts as a router. Requires `multi_clustering__reportree__input` parameter to be one of ['alleles', 'alignment', 'vcf'] to select the correct logic path.

components.description: ReporTree pipeline variant designed specifically for VCF inputs. Constructs a GrapeTree Minimum Spanning Tree from genetic distance (Hamming) matrices. Features optional Hierarchical Clustering (HC) and Augur export for Auspice visualization.
use_cases: Generate an MST directly from multi-sample VCF files
Cluster samples based on SNP distances
Perform hierarchical clustering on variant data
components.notes: Extracts sample names dynamically from file names. Capable of pinpointing the closest samples using a specific sample of interest threshold.

components.description: FDA's CFSAN SNP Pipeline for identifying SNPs in sequence data. Maps reads to a reference, constructs a multi-FASTA SNP matrix, infers phylogenetic trees using IQ-TREE, and integrates with Augur to export Auspice JSONs.
use_cases: Run the FDA CFSAN SNP pipeline on sequence data
Identify SNPs and build an IQ-TREE phylogeny
Generate a SNP matrix from raw sequence reads
components.notes: Supports container versioning ('2.2.1' or '2.0.2') directly through the params payload. Automatically prunes output alignment artifacts.

components.description: ReporTree pipeline variant for FASTA sequences. Automatically performs Multiple Sequence Alignment (MSA) using MAFFT before calculating distance matrices and generating GrapeTree/Hierarchical clustering outputs. Includes Augur integration.
use_cases: Align multi-sample FASTA sequences and build a cluster tree
Perform MAFFT alignment followed by ReporTree clustering
components.notes: Utilizes MAFFT (`--auto`) prior to standard ReporTree execution.

components.description: ReporTree pipeline variant for allelic profiles (cgMLST). Merges multi-sample profiles using chewBBACA, calculates GrapeTree/Hierarchical clusters, and generates Auspice-compatible outputs via Augur.
use_cases: Cluster samples using cgMLST allelic profiles
Merge chewBBACA profiles and generate an MST
components.notes: Leverages `chewie ExtractCgMLST` for profile consolidation before distance matrix computation.

components.description: Creates a Minimum Spanning Tree (MST) from a collection of VCF files. Generates a Newick tree structure, a Hamming distance matrix, and a cgMLST-style distances CSV.
use_cases: Convert VCF files directly into a Minimum Spanning Tree
Calculate a Hamming distance matrix from variants
components.notes: Employs `cgmlst-dists` to compute pairwise distance metrics natively from the generated matrix.

components.description: Constructs a pangenome graph from annotated GFF files. Filters out invalid genes, performs core genome alignment using MAFFT, and resolves paralogs. Suitable for large multi-sample bacterial cohorts.
components.notes: Strictly utilizes MAFFT for the core alignment phase. Tolerates custom thresholds for clustering stringency.

components.description: Standalone GrapeTree pipeline for cgMLST allelic profiles. Merges profiles using chewBBACA ExtractCgMLST, calculates RapidNJ Newick trees, computes dists, and processes metadata via Augur to generate Auspice JSON output.
use_cases: Build a GrapeTree from cgMLST profiles
Generate a RapidNJ tree from allele data
components.notes: Produces both standard GrapeTree output and a RapidNJ-specific tree. Includes `cgmlst-dists` evaluation.

components.description: Core genome SNP calling pipeline. Aligns sequence reads (Illumina paired-end, IonTorrent single-end) or direct FASTA assemblies against a reference using Snippy, then aggregates multiple VCF results into a core-SNP matrix using Snippy-Core.
components.notes: Automatically toggles parameters for single vs paired end reads, and accepts pre-computed VCF directories to run Snippy-Core exclusively.

components.description: Reference-free SNP discovery and phylogenetic analysis tool. Constructs k-mer based SNP matrices (Core or All), builds Maximum Likelihood trees with IQ-TREE, processes with GrapeTree, and exports an Auspice JSON using Augur.
use_cases: Perform reference-free SNP discovery
Build a tree based on k-mer SNP matrices
components.notes: Toggles between generating a Core-SNP matrix or All-SNPs matrix depending on the `--analysis_type` parameter. Substitutes dashes for dots in sequence headers to comply with IQ-TREE requirements.

components.description: Performs quality control checks on raw sequence data using FastQC. Generates comprehensive HTML reports and zip archives of QC metrics.
use_cases: Check the quality of Illumina sequence reads
Generate standard FastQC reports for raw data
components.notes: Specifically gated to run only on Illumina paired-end and Ion Torrent data. Outputs are dynamically renamed with the execution timestamp.

components.description: Calculates statistics and generates interactive HTML summary plots for long-read sequencing data using NanoPlot.
use_cases: Generate QC plots for Nanopore long reads
Evaluate ONT read length and quality statistics
components.notes: Restricted to Nanopore sequencing data. Runs with '--tsv_stats' and '--no_static' flags to optimize for web rendering and parsing.

components.description: Evaluates genome assembly quality. Runs QUAST on input assemblies, extracts key metrics using shell utilities, and aggregates results across multiple samples into a single summary TSV.
use_cases: Evaluate genome assembly quality metrics
Calculate N50 and contig statistics for assemblies with QUAST
components.notes: Includes a secondary 'summary' process running on Ubuntu to concatenate individual sample TSVs into a master summary report. Uses the '--fast' flag for rapid evaluation.
